raw_text.response:
Let  

\[
v=\begin{bmatrix}9\\0\\0\\0\end{bmatrix},\qquad 
u_{1}= \begin{bmatrix}2\\2\\1\\0\end{bmatrix},\qquad 
u_{2}= \begin{bmatrix}-2\\2\\0\\1\end{bmatrix}.
\]

The subspace we project onto is  

\[
\mathcal{U}= \operatorname{span}\{u_{1},u_{2}\}\subset\mathbb R^{4}.
\]

For a subspace spanned by the columns of a matrix \(A\) the orthogonal projection of a vector \(v\) onto \(\operatorname{col}(A)\) is  

\[
P_{\mathcal U}v = A\,(A^{\!T}A)^{-1}A^{\!T}v .
\]

---

### 1. Form the matrix \(A\)

\[
A=\begin{bmatrix}
2 & -2\\
2 &  2\\
1 &  0\\
0 &  1
\end{bmatrix},
\qquad\text{(columns are }u_{1},u_{2}\text{).}
\]

### 2. Compute \(A^{\!T}A\)

\[
A^{\!T}A=
\begin{bmatrix}
u_{1}\!\cdot\!u_{1} & u_{1}\!\cdot\!u_{2}\\[2pt]
u_{2}\!\cdot\!u_{1} & u_{2}\!\cdot\!u_{2}
\end{bmatrix}
=
\begin{bmatrix}
9 & 0\\
0 & 9
\end{bmatrix}=9I_{2}.
\]

Hence  

\[
(A^{\!T}A)^{-1}= \frac1{9}I_{2}.
\]

### 3. Compute \(A^{\!T}v\)

\[
A^{\!T}v=
\begin{bmatrix}
u_{1}\!\cdot\!v\\[2pt]
u_{2}\!\cdot\!v
\end{bmatrix}
=
\begin{bmatrix}
2\cdot 9+2\cdot0+1\cdot0+0\cdot0\\[2pt]
(-2)\cdot9+2\cdot0+0\cdot0+1\cdot0
\end{bmatrix}
=
\begin{bmatrix}
18\\-18
\end{bmatrix}.
\]

### 4. Form the coefficient vector  

\[
c=(A^{\!T}A)^{-1}A^{\!T}v
   =\frac1{9}\begin{bmatrix}18\\-18\end{bmatrix}
   =\begin{bmatrix}2\\-2\end{bmatrix}.
\]

Thus the projection is  

\[
P_{\mathcal U}v = A\,c = 2u_{1}-2u_{2}.
\]

### 5. Evaluate  

\[
2u_{1}= \begin{bmatrix}4\\4\\2\\0\end{bmatrix},\qquad
-2u_{2}= \begin{bmatrix}4\\-4\\0\\-2\end{bmatrix},
\]

\[
P_{\mathcal U}v = 
\begin{bmatrix}4\\4\\2\\0\end{bmatrix}
+
\begin{bmatrix}4\\-4\\0\\-2\end{bmatrix}
=
\boxed{\begin{bmatrix}8\\0\\2\\-2\end{bmatrix}}.
\]

---

**Check:**  

\(v-P_{\mathcal U}v = (1,0,-2,2)\) is orthogonal to both \(u_{1}\) and \(u_{2}\):

\[
(1,0,-2,2)\!\cdot\!u_{1}=2-2=0,\qquad
(1,0,-2,2)\!\cdot\!u_{2}=-2+2=0.
\]

Hence \([8,0,2,-2]\) is indeed the orthogonal projection of \([9,0,0,0]\) onto the space spanned by the two filter vectors.
